name: redux-expert
description: Redux and Redux Toolkit state management expert for enterprise React PWA applications, covering store setup, slices, async thunks, state persistence, middleware, DevTools, and JSON-driven UI integration

can_do: Implement Redux Toolkit store with configureStore
Implement Redux Saga for complex async flows
cannot_do: Decide when to use Redux vs alternatives (opinion)

redux_definition.statement: Redux is a predictable state container with three principles: single source of truth (one store), state is read-only (dispatch actions to change), changes via pure functions (reducers)

redux_toolkit.statement: Redux Toolkit is the official, opinionated, batteries-included toolset for Redux. It includes configureStore, createSlice, createAsyncThunk, and reduces boilerplate by 80%. Always use Redux Toolkit over vanilla Redux

configure_store.statement: configureStore() from Redux Toolkit sets up Redux store with good defaults: Redux DevTools enabled, Redux Thunk middleware included, immutability checks in dev. Syntax: configureStore({ reducer: rootReducer })
configure_store.example: const store = configureStore({ reducer: { cases: casesReducer, user: userReducer } });

create_slice.statement: createSlice() generates action creators and reducers together. Takes name, initialState, reducers object. Returns slice with .actions and .reducer. Eliminates action type constants and switch statements
create_slice.example: const casesSlice = createSlice({ name: 'cases', initialState, reducers: { addCase: (state, action) => { state.items.push(action.payload); } } });

immer_integration.statement: Redux Toolkit uses Immer internally, allowing you to write 'mutating' logic in reducers. state.items.push() is actually safe and creates immutable update

create_async_thunk.statement: createAsyncThunk() generates thunk that dispatches pending/fulfilled/rejected actions automatically. Syntax: createAsyncThunk('actionType', async (arg, thunkAPI) => { return await fetch() })
create_async_thunk.example: export const fetchCases = createAsyncThunk('cases/fetch', async (filters) => { const response = await fetch('/api/cases'); return response.json(); });

extra_reducers.statement: Handle async thunk states in slice extraReducers using builder.addCase(). Lifecycle: .pending, .fulfilled, .rejected

redux_thunk.statement: Redux Thunk middleware included by default in Redux Toolkit. Allows action creators to return functions instead of actions. Good for simple async logic

redux_saga.statement: Redux Saga uses ES6 generators for complex async flows. Better for: cancellation, debouncing, racing, sequential/parallel tasks, complex error handling. Not included by default, must add middleware

use_selector.statement: useSelector(selector) hook extracts data from Redux store. Subscribes to store updates. Returns data from selector function. Selector receives full state: useSelector((state) => state.cases.items)

use_dispatch.statement: useDispatch() hook returns dispatch function to dispatch actions. const dispatch = useDispatch(); dispatch(fetchCases())

provider.statement: Wrap app in <Provider store={store}> to make Redux store available to all components

redux_persist.statement: redux-persist saves Redux state to storage (localStorage, sessionStorage, IndexedDB) and rehydrates on app load. Essential for PWA offline functionality

persist_config.statement: persistConfig defines: key (storage key), storage (storage engine), whitelist (reducers to persist), blacklist (reducers to exclude), transforms (encryption, compression)

encryption.statement: Use redux-persist-transform-encrypt to encrypt persisted state (critical for PII). Creates encrypted transform with secretKey

middleware_definition.statement: Redux middleware is (store) => (next) => (action) => { /* logic */ return next(action); }. Intercepts actions before they reach reducers. Use for: logging, analytics, API calls, crash reporting

redux_devtools.statement: Redux DevTools browser extension provides: time-travel debugging (replay actions), state inspection, action history, export/import state, dispatch actions manually. Enabled by default in Redux Toolkit dev mode

time_travel.statement: Time-travel debugging lets you replay past actions, jump to any state, and see how actions affect state changes. Critical for debugging complex state flows

reselect.statement: Reselect library (re-exported from Redux Toolkit as createSelector) creates memoized selectors. Only recomputes when input selectors' values change. Prevents unnecessary re-renders

create_selector.statement: createSelector([input selectors], resultFunction). Result only recomputes when inputs change. Example: createSelector([selectCases, selectFilters], (cases, filters) => cases.filter(...))
create_selector.example: const selectFilteredCases = createSelector([selectAllCases, selectFilters], (cases, filters) => cases.filter(c => c.status === filters.status));

create_entity_adapter.statement: createEntityAdapter() provides CRUD methods for normalized state: addOne, addMany, updateOne, removeOne. Maintains items as { ids: [], entities: {} }. Includes selectors: selectAll, selectById, selectIds

normalized_benefits.statement: Normalized state benefits: no duplication, easier updates (update by ID), better performance (no nested searches), consistent data structure

typed_hooks.statement: Create typed hooks for TypeScript: export const useAppDispatch = () => useDispatch<AppDispatch>(); export const useAppSelector: TypedUseSelectorHook<RootState> = useSelector;
typed_hooks.example: const cases = useAppSelector((state) => state.cases.items); // Fully typed

root_state_type.statement: Infer RootState type: export type RootState = ReturnType<typeof store.getState>; Infer AppDispatch: export type AppDispatch = typeof store.dispatch;

feature_structure.statement: Structure Redux by feature, not by type. Each feature folder contains: slice, selectors, components. Example: features/cases/casesSlice.js, features/user/userSlice.js

minimal_state.statement: Keep state minimal. Don't store: derived data (use selectors), UI state (use local component state), non-serializable values (functions, Promises, class instances)

use_toolkit.statement: Always use Redux Toolkit, not vanilla Redux. Toolkit reduces boilerplate by 80%, includes best practices, and has better DX
